select testdb.t1.id AS id,testdb.t1.a AS a,testdb.t1.b AS b,testdb.t1.c AS c,testdb.t1.d AS d
Each row = one plan operation. Indentation shows parent→child (outer→inner). Self = time in this op only; Total = including children.
Click ▾/▸ to collapse/expand a subtree. Click a row to pin its details. Ctrl+F to search.
Orange rows ⚠ have a warning in the Query Analysis panel below.
index_merge=on — Uses two or more index range scans on the same table and combines row IDs (union/intersection/sort_union) instead of falling back to a full scan. A composite index
index_merge_union=on — Union variant of index_merge: OR'ed predicates are satisfied by scanning each index separately, then UNION'ing their row IDs.
